text:
Market today: Chip stocks started Q3 with a pullback after a big Q2 run - that headline is the real driver for your book today. Semis cooling off after a hot streak is a normal exhale, but on margin it stings faster.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest alarm - it's a 3x leveraged ETF already deep in the hole and only ~$17 above your $200 stop; decay eats you alive the longer this drags. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already kissing your $21.38 stop - one bad tick and you're out, margin amplifies that. SMH (-2.5%) is the cleaner semiconductor bet but still underwater and today's chip dud headline isn't helping.

Watch: Whether SMH bounces or continues to roll over - that tells you if this is a one-day chip hiccup or something broader. Also watch if SPAL holds $21.38; it's on the edge right now.

Bottom line: Your leveraged positions are the fire - tend those before looking at anything new.
plays: SOXL: At -14.52% on a 3x leveraged ETF, decay is working against you daily. Respect the $200 stop hard - no averaging down here.
SPAL: Already at -13.72% and essentially sitting on your $21.38 stop. Decide now whether you're in or out - don't let a new position bleed on margin.
No new buys today - chip sector opened Q3 weak, your leveraged names need attention first before adding any exposure.
